---
title: "Quarto for Manuscripts"
subtitle: "YAML, cross-references, citations, and inline reporting"
---

```{r}
#| output: false
library(dplyr)
df_clean <- palmerpenguins::penguins |>
  filter(!is.na(sex), !is.na(body_mass_g)) |>
  mutate(sex = factor(sex, labels = c("Female", "Male")))
```

## The manuscript document

A Quarto manuscript lives in a `.qmd` file. The YAML header controls everything: output format, bibliography, author details, and rendering options.

A minimal manuscript header:

```yaml
---
title: "The Effect of Species on Penguin Body Mass"
author:
  - name: "Your Name"
    affiliation: "Your Institution"
date: today
format:
  pdf:
    documentclass: apa7
    bibliography: references.bib
    csl: apa.csl
  docx:
    reference-doc: custom-reference.docx
    bibliography: references.bib
    csl: apa.csl
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
editor: source
---
```

## Cross-references

Label figures and tables in chunk options, then cite them with `@`:

````{{markdown}}
```{{r}}
#| label: fig-mass
#| fig-cap: "Body mass by species."
ggplot(...)
```
````

In prose: `See @fig-mass for the full distribution.` → renders as "See Figure 1".

For tables:

````{{markdown}}
```{{r}}
#| label: tbl-demo
#| tbl-cap: "Sample characteristics."
gtsummary_table
```
````

`As shown in @tbl-demo, ...` → "As shown in Table 1, ..."

::: callout-important
Cross-reference labels must start with the correct prefix: `fig-` for figures, `tbl-` for tables, `sec-` for sections, `eq-` for equations.
:::

## Citations

Add a `references.bib` file at the project root. Cite with `@key` (in-text) or `[@key]` (parenthetical):

```{{markdown}}
Mixed-effects models were fitted following @Bates2015.
Beta regression is appropriate for bounded outcomes [@Ferrari2004].
```

Quarto handles the bibliography automatically based on your CSL style.

### Getting .bib entries

- Export directly from Zotero (recommended)
- Use `citr` RStudio add-in for in-editor citation search
- Paste from Google Scholar → Cite → BibTeX

## Inline reporting

Embed computed values directly in prose so they update automatically when data or models change.
For example, in your `.qmd` file you might write:
```markdown
The sample comprised `r nrow(df_clean)` participants
(M~age~ = `r round(mean(df_clean$age), 1)`, SD = `r round(sd(df_clean$age), 1)`).
```

For model results, define a small helper in `R/utils.R`:
```r
report_t <- function(model, term) {
  b   <- broom::tidy(model, conf.int = TRUE)
  row <- b[b$term == term, ]
  glue::glue(
    "b = {round(row$estimate, 2)}, 95% CI [{round(row$conf.low, 2)}, {round(row$conf.high, 2)}], ",
    "t({round(broom::glance(model)$df.residual)}) = {round(row$statistic, 2)}, ",
    "p {ifelse(row$p.value < .001, '< .001', paste0('= ', round(row$p.value, 3)))}"
  )
}
```

Then use it inline:

```markdown
The effect of sex was significant (`r report_t(m2, "sexMale")`).
```

## Equations

Write LaTeX math inline with `$...$` or in display blocks with `$$...$$`:

```{{markdown}}
The model was specified as:

$$y_i = \beta_0 + \beta_1 \text{Species}_i + \beta_2 \text{Sex}_i + \varepsilon_i$$
```

## Section references

Add `#sec-` labels to headings and cross-reference them:

```{{markdown}}
## Results {#sec-results}

See @sec-results for the full analysis.
```

## Chapter slides

```{=html}
<a href="../slides/07_manuscripts.html" class="btn btn-outline-primary" target="_blank">
  Open slides ↗
</a>
```
